author: g1.eth
content:
Hii guys , Im a protocol engg for some years now , Now I want to go a deeper into AI , agents and stuff , I know a little late but thats fine I can catch up ,I'm seeing a lot of people even here , building AI bots, agents, workflow automations, and multi-agent systems. If you were starting today, what learning path would you follow? Any resources, courses, projects, or frameworks you'd recommend? ,

One thing to clarify: I'm not necessarily looking to become an "Agentic AI Engineer." My goal is to leverage AI to become significantly more productive and build useful things, rather than just using tools like Claude, Antigravity, or other assistants at the surface level. 

Looking for high-signal advice, which is why I'm posting here. Thanks!

author: sky.cactus
content:
My advice: Support open source and open weight.  Use something like OpenCode or Pi with Minimax M3, GLM 5.1 (soon 5.2), or Kimi K2.7.  If you want to go hardcore, use Qwen 3.6:27b locally.

This is still basically just "use existing tools" though.  If you want to build your own tools I recommend first using existing tools for a while and then think about how you could design tools to engineer yourself out of your new job of LLM middle manager.
